repro.constraint: SYNTHETIC (PHASE 3/4b validated generator) + 2020 calibration/OOS ONLY; never 2026/other; LONGSHORT = RESEARCH (deploy needs LO-exception sign-off)

verdict.headline: THE MULTI-SLEEVE BOOK does NOT robustly beat the best single sleeve on cross-regime worst-case (0/6 cadences) -- the honest finding is that naive equal-risk blending of ['TREND', 'MR', 'LONGSHORT', 'VOLTGT_DEF'] does not add cross-regime robustness over the best component at most TFs. The LONGSHORT engine still provides the bear protection (best at 30m), but the book construction needs refinement (regime-routing, not a static mix). SHORT = RESEARCH.
lines: HEADLINE: THE MULTI-SLEEVE BOOK does NOT robustly beat the best single sleeve on cross-regime worst-case (0/6 cadences) -- the honest finding is that naive equal-risk blending of ['TREND', 'MR', 'LONGSHORT', 'VOLTGT_DEF'] does not add cross-regime robustness over the best component at most TFs. The LONGSHORT engine still provides the bear protection (best at 30m), but the book construction needs refinement (regime-routing, not a static mix). SHORT = RESEARCH.
Q1: THE LONGSHORT-MA ENGINE -- bear protection WITHOUT a crippling bull drag, per TF?
(LS = longshort engine at 20.0bps/yr borrow; bull-drag = LS bull net - trend bull net;
bear-protect = how much LESS-negative LS's bear maxDD is vs trend-alone)
1d   : bull-drag -2.01pp (TOLERABLE); bear-protect +0.53pp DD; bear net -0.55%; mix net 3.4%
4h   : bull-drag -0.91pp (TOLERABLE); bear-protect +-0.04pp DD; bear net 0.91%; mix net 2.0%
2h   : bull-drag -0.8pp (TOLERABLE); bear-protect +-0.01pp DD; bear net 1.22%; mix net 2.1%
1h   : bull-drag -1.1600000000000001pp (TOLERABLE); bear-protect +0.29pp DD; bear net 2.53%; mix net 2.8%
30m  : bull-drag -1.09pp (TOLERABLE); bear-protect +0.43pp DD; bear net 2.25%; mix net 2.6%
15m  : bull-drag -1.09pp (TOLERABLE); bear-protect +0.43pp DD; bear net 2.25%; mix net 2.6%
-> BEST TF FOR LONGSHORT (bear-protection + worst-regime net, drag-penalized): 30m
Q2: SHORT-BORROW SENSITIVITY -- engine net at 0/10/20/30 bps/yr (does the edge survive cost?):
1d   : 0bps: bull -0.95/bear -0.55/stitch 11.77  |  10bps: bull -0.95/bear -0.55/stitch 11.77  |  20bps: bull -0.95/bear -0.55/stitch 11.76  |  30bps: bull -0.95/bear -0.55/stitch 11.75
4h   : 0bps: bull -0.11/bear 0.91/stitch 5.9  |  10bps: bull -0.11/bear 0.91/stitch 5.9  |  20bps: bull -0.11/bear 0.91/stitch 5.89  |  30bps: bull -0.11/bear 0.91/stitch 5.89
2h   : 0bps: bull -0.14/bear 1.22/stitch 6.15  |  10bps: bull -0.14/bear 1.22/stitch 6.15  |  20bps: bull -0.14/bear 1.22/stitch 6.15  |  30bps: bull -0.14/bear 1.22/stitch 6.15
1h   : 0bps: bull -0.23/bear 2.53/stitch 7.32  |  10bps: bull -0.23/bear 2.53/stitch 7.32  |  20bps: bull -0.23/bear 2.53/stitch 7.32  |  30bps: bull -0.23/bear 2.53/stitch 7.32
30m  : 0bps: bull -0.32/bear 2.25/stitch 7.05  |  10bps: bull -0.32/bear 2.25/stitch 7.05  |  20bps: bull -0.32/bear 2.25/stitch 7.05  |  30bps: bull -0.32/bear 2.25/stitch 7.05
15m  : 0bps: bull -0.32/bear 2.25/stitch 7.05  |  10bps: bull -0.32/bear 2.25/stitch 7.05  |  20bps: bull -0.32/bear 2.25/stitch 7.05  |  30bps: bull -0.32/bear 2.25/stitch 7.05
-> borrow cost of 0->30bps/yr on the stitched cycle = 0.01pp net (small: the longshort engine is NOT borrow-fragile)
Q3: IS THE LONGSHORT ENGINE NET-POSITIVE OR NET-NEUTRAL (full-cycle stitched, after borrow)?
1d   : stitched LS net 11.76% (worst -2.2%) vs trend 38.52% -> NET-POSITIVE; beats cost-matched null by 9.98pp
4h   : stitched LS net 5.89% (worst -2.5%) vs trend 15.65% -> NET-POSITIVE; beats cost-matched null by 4.77pp
2h   : stitched LS net 6.15% (worst -2.8%) vs trend 15.98% -> NET-POSITIVE; beats cost-matched null by 5.15pp
1h   : stitched LS net 7.32% (worst -3.9%) vs trend 18.83% -> NET-POSITIVE; beats cost-matched null by 6.13pp
30m  : stitched LS net 7.05% (worst -4.1%) vs trend 18.7% -> NET-POSITIVE; beats cost-matched null by 5.86pp
15m  : stitched LS net 7.05% (worst -4.1%) vs trend 18.7% -> NET-POSITIVE; beats cost-matched null by 5.86pp
Q4 (DECISIVE): THE MULTI-SLEEVE BOOK vs EVERY SINGLE SLEEVE -- the cross-regime robustness gain:
1d   : book worst-regime net -4.9% / full-mix-worst DD -9.5% / worst p05 -9.22%; beats EVERY single on [worst-net False, worst-DD True, p05 False]; gain vs best single: net -3.95pp, DD 2.3pp
4h   : book worst-regime net -1.98% / full-mix-worst DD -7.2% / worst p05 -4.46%; beats EVERY single on [worst-net False, worst-DD False, p05 False]; gain vs best single: net -1.87pp, DD -1.0pp
2h   : book worst-regime net -1.82% / full-mix-worst DD -7.1% / worst p05 -4.31%; beats EVERY single on [worst-net False, worst-DD False, p05 False]; gain vs best single: net -1.68pp, DD -1.5pp
1h   : book worst-regime net -1.8% / full-mix-worst DD -8.2% / worst p05 -4.69%; beats EVERY single on [worst-net False, worst-DD False, p05 False]; gain vs best single: net -1.57pp, DD -2.4pp
30m  : book worst-regime net -1.98% / full-mix-worst DD -9.4% / worst p05 -5.0%; beats EVERY single on [worst-net False, worst-DD False, p05 False]; gain vs best single: net -1.66pp, DD -3.1pp
15m  : book worst-regime net -1.98% / full-mix-worst DD -9.4% / worst p05 -5.0%; beats EVERY single on [worst-net False, worst-DD False, p05 False]; gain vs best single: net -1.66pp, DD -3.1pp
Q4b: DEPLOYABLE-TODAY (long-only: trend+MR+voltgt) vs RESEARCH-FULL (with longshort) book:
1d   : long-only book worst-net -7.46% / worst-DD -16.7%  vs  full book worst-net -4.9% / worst-DD -9.5%  -> longshort adds worst-net 2.56pp, worst-DD 7.2pp [RESEARCH]
4h   : long-only book worst-net -3.95% / worst-DD -12.4%  vs  full book worst-net -1.98% / worst-DD -7.2%  -> longshort adds worst-net 1.97pp, worst-DD 5.2pp [RESEARCH]
2h   : long-only book worst-net -3.89% / worst-DD -12.4%  vs  full book worst-net -1.82% / worst-DD -7.1%  -> longshort adds worst-net 2.07pp, worst-DD 5.3pp [RESEARCH]
1h   : long-only book worst-net -4.71% / worst-DD -17.3%  vs  full book worst-net -1.8% / worst-DD -8.2%  -> longshort adds worst-net 2.91pp, worst-DD 9.1pp [RESEARCH]
30m  : long-only book worst-net -4.78% / worst-DD -18.2%  vs  full book worst-net -1.98% / worst-DD -9.4%  -> longshort adds worst-net 2.8pp, worst-DD 8.8pp [RESEARCH]
15m  : long-only book worst-net -4.78% / worst-DD -18.2%  vs  full book worst-net -1.98% / worst-DD -9.4%  -> longshort adds worst-net 2.8pp, worst-DD 8.8pp [RESEARCH]
Q5: 2020-REAL OOS (Oct-Dec BULL) anchor -- the longshort bull-drag on REAL data:
1d   : LONGSHORT OOS net 5.4% (DD -5.9) vs TREND 13.6% (DD -11.0) -> real bull-drag -8.2pp
4h   : LONGSHORT OOS net 4.8% (DD -3.9) vs TREND 19.3% (DD -6.2) -> real bull-drag -14.5pp
2h   : LONGSHORT OOS net 7.4% (DD -8.3) vs TREND 34.6% (DD -5.6) -> real bull-drag -27.2pp
1h   : LONGSHORT OOS net 17.4% (DD -4.1) vs TREND 50.4% (DD -5.8) -> real bull-drag -33.0pp
30m  : LONGSHORT OOS net 12.8% (DD -4.6) vs TREND 46.6% (DD -7.9) -> real bull-drag -33.8pp
15m  : LONGSHORT OOS net 13.8% (DD -6.9) vs TREND 54.6% (DD -11.5) -> real bull-drag -40.8pp

CAVEATS (binding): (1) SYNTHETIC stress surface from PHASE 3/4b's VALIDATED generator (2020-calibrated stylized facts ONLY) + the 2020 real OOS -- NOT real future data. (2) The LONGSHORT engine VIOLATES long-only+spot -> RESEARCH; deploy needs the user's explicit LO-exception sign-off. (3) Short-borrow MODELLED at 0/10/20/30 bps/yr (prorated per-bar on the short leg); real borrow can spike in a squeeze (the 30bps case is the stress). (4) maker cost, no MtM double-count, lag-1 causal; the cost-matched random-direction NULL controls for the cost convention. (5) >=20 seeds; distributions (mean +- spread + WORST seed) reported. (6) PRE-REGISTERED equal-risk weights on a held-aside synthetic-BULL slice (NOT fit on the stress surface). (7) The synthetic bear is the 2020-COVID fast-V-crash exemplar; a slow grind-down bear may differ.
